name: OSPF Area Mismatch
issue: OSPF Adjacency Failure due to Area Mismatch
possible_causes: Mismatched OSPF area configuration on connecting interfaces
Network statement misconfiguration under router ospf
remediation: Modify OSPF area configuration on the interfaces of both routers so they match (e.g., area 0 on both sides).

name: OSPF Authentication Mismatch
issue: OSPF Adjacency Failure due to Authentication Mismatch
possible_causes: Mismatched MD5 authentication keys
One side is configured for MD5 authentication while the other is not
remediation: Sync OSPF authentication password keys on both interfaces. Check if both sides have 'ip ospf authentication message-digest' and 'ip ospf message-digest-key 1 md5 <key>'.

name: Interface Administratively Shutdown
possible_causes: The interface is explicitly shut down via the 'shutdown' command
New interface provisioned but not enabled yet
remediation: Enter interface configuration mode and execute the 'no shutdown' command to enable the port.

name: Missing Static Route
issue: Routing Path Failure due to Missing Static Route
possible_causes: Static route to target subnet was deleted or never added on transit routers
Dynamic routing protocol did not redistribute static routes
remediation: Configure a static route using 'ip route <subnet> <mask} <next_hop>' or configure dynamic routing to advertise the subnet.

name: Traffic Blocked by Access Control List
issue: Security Access Denied by ACL Policy
possible_causes: An access control list (ACL) contains a deny statement matching the source/destination traffic
Implicit deny at the end of the access control list blocking traffic
remediation: Modify the ACL to permit the required traffic, or bind the correct ACL to the interface.

name: Control Plane High CPU Exhaustion
possible_causes: Excessive network interrupts due to packet forwarding issues (IP Input)
OSPF SPF calculation loop or routing loops
Broadcast storm or heavy SSH/snmp pollers
remediation: Identify the high CPU process. If it is 'IP Input', verify if fast switching / CEF is enabled. If 'OSPF', check for flap networks. Rate-limit control plane traffic using CoPP.

name: Low Free Processor Memory
issue: System Low Free Memory / Buffer Leak
possible_causes: Memory leak in system process
Oversized routing table for device hardware memory profile
remediation: Review show memory statistics. Upgrade system RAM, optimize routing table sizing (prefix lists/summarization), or upgrade IOS firmware to resolve potential memory leak.

name: OSPF MTU Mismatch
issue: OSPF Stuck in EXSTART/EXCHANGE State due to MTU Mismatch
possible_causes: Mismatched Maximum Transmission Unit (MTU) sizes on connecting interfaces
remediation: Adjust interface MTU sizes to match on both sides using 'ip mtu <size>' or use 'ip ospf mtu-ignore' under interface config.

name: OSPF Hello/Dead Timer Mismatch
issue: OSPF Adjacency Failure due to Timer Mismatch
possible_causes: Mismatched hello-interval or dead-interval values on connecting interfaces
remediation: Align hello and dead intervals on both interfaces using 'ip ospf hello-interval' and 'ip ospf dead-interval'.

name: Duplex Mismatch
possible_causes: One side is configured as half-duplex and the other side is configured as full-duplex
remediation: Configure both ends of the Ethernet link to auto-negotiate, or hardcode both to full duplex.

name: Input Queue Drops
possible_causes: Device receiving packets faster than the CPU can process them
Lack of hardware CEF caching
remediation: Verify that CEF (Cisco Express Forwarding) is enabled globally. Rate limit incoming traffic or configure QoS queuing policies.

name: High CRC Errors
possible_causes: Faulty physical Ethernet cable or transceiver SFP
Duplex mismatch or electromagnetic interference
remediation: Swap physical cables and transceiver modules. Ensure duplex match.

name: Routing Loop Detected
possible_causes: Incorrect static routes pointing back to upstream routers
Redistribution loops between OSPF and BGP
remediation: Analyze path via traceroute, remove duplicate static routes, and implement route filters / tag-based filtering on redistribution nodes.

name: VLAN Pruning Mismatch
possible_causes: Required VLAN is not allowed or was pruned on the 802.1Q trunk interface
VLAN does not exist in the switch database
remediation: Create the VLAN in global config and allow it on the trunk link using 'switchport trunk allowed vlan add <vlan_id>'.

name: NAT Pool Exhausted
possible_causes: NAT pool too small for active network users
Lack of Port Address Translation (PAT/overload) configuration
remediation: Increase the NAT pool address range, decrease translation timeouts, or configure PAT overload on the external interface.

name: Transceiver RX Power Low
issue: Optical SFP Signal Loss (Low RX Power)
possible_causes: Dirty fiber optic patch cable connector
Failing optical transceiver module
remediation: Clean fiber connectors using optical cleaning swabs. Replace the fiber patch cable or the SFP transceiver.

name: Next Hop Unreachable
possible_causes: Layer 2 ARP resolution failure for next hop IP
Direct interface connected to the next hop is down
remediation: Ensure physical interface is up, and verify basic ping reachability to next-hop IP. Check ARP database.

name: BGP Neighbor Down
possible_causes: TCP port 179 blocked by firewall or ACL
Mismatched AS numbers, peer IP, or password authentication
remediation: Check ping connectivity. Verify BGP peer configuration. Ensure TCP port 179 is allowed in ACLs.

name: DHCP Pool Exhaustion
issue: Dynamic IP Allocation Failure (DHCP Pool Exhausted)
possible_causes: Address range in DHCP pool is smaller than actual network client density
Lease time set too high causing address holding
remediation: Expand DHCP network scope pool range, reduce lease durations, and clear conflicts using 'clear ip dhcp binding *'.

name: SSH Login Brute Force
possible_causes: Active scan or script trying to guess SSH logins on the device
remediation: Implement Line VTY access limits using ACL, restrict SSH ports using Control Plane Policing (CoPP), or configure authentication blockout times.

name: OSPF Area Type Mismatch
possible_causes: One router is configured for Stub/NSSA area while the neighbor is configured for normal Area
remediation: Ensure both OSPF routers have the exact same area flags (e.g. stub or nssa) under 'router ospf'.

name: OSPF Router ID Conflict
possible_causes: Two routers in the same OSPF domain share the same router-id
remediation: Manually configure a unique router ID using the 'router-id <id>' command in router OSPF configuration mode.

name: Gigabit Ethernet Link Flapping
possible_causes: Defective hardware interface port or physical patch cord cable
Unstable link negotiation or SFP issue
remediation: Replace physical cables. Turn off auto-negotiation if it's unstable, and configure hardcoded speed and duplex.

name: Default Route Missing
possible_causes: Default route 0.0.0.0/0 not propagated or deleted on the gateway router
remediation: Add static default route pointing to ISP next hop, or propagate OSPF default route using 'default-information originate'.

name: Unsecure Protocol Active
possible_causes: Telnet is enabled on Line VTY ports instead of SSH only
remediation: Disable Telnet and force SSH using 'transport input ssh' under Line VTY 0 4 configuration.

name: DHCP Rogue Server Detected
possible_causes: Unauthorized DHCP server plugged into client VLAN broadcasting incorrect gateways
remediation: Enable DHCP Snooping globally and configure ports to trusted/untrusted to block unauthorized DHCP responses.

name: Inside Interface Missing NAT Configuration
possible_causes: The 'ip nat inside' statement is missing on the client-facing local interface
remediation: Access interface configuration for client subnet and enter the 'ip nat inside' command.

name: Outside Interface Missing NAT Configuration
possible_causes: The 'ip nat outside' statement is missing on the WAN external interface
remediation: Access interface configuration for the external WAN and enter the 'ip nat outside' command.

name: Native VLAN Mismatch
possible_causes: Trunk port native VLAN is configured differently on local and neighbor switches
remediation: Change Native VLAN on trunk interface to match using 'switchport trunk native vlan <id>'.

name: SNMP Community String Default
possible_causes: Device configured with common default strings like 'public' or 'private'
remediation: Remove default strings and create custom secure strings with read-only/write access permissions.

name: BGP Path MTU Discovery Issue
possible_causes: MTU fragmentation drops TCP packets during large BGP table updates
remediation: Configure TCP path MTU discovery under BGP configuration or hardcode lower TCP MSS values.

name: OSPF Broadcast Network No DR Elected
possible_causes: All router interfaces in the subnet are configured with priority 0
remediation: Change OSPF priority on at least one router interface to a value greater than 0 using 'ip ospf priority <val>'.

name: OSPF Passive Interface Blocking Hellos
possible_causes: Connecting interface between routers is set to 'passive-interface' under OSPF
remediation: Remove passive interface command on connecting link using 'no passive-interface <if_name>' under OSPF config.

name: Suboptimal Route due to Admin Distance
possible_causes: Static route overshadowing dynamic OSPF route due to default lower AD (1 vs 110)
remediation: Configure floating static route with AD greater than 110 (e.g. 120) or fix metric advertisements in OSPF.

name: FIB Table Overflow
possible_causes: Device hardware TCAM table exhausted due to too many routing prefixes
remediation: Summarize routing table prefixes or filter unnecessary external paths at boundaries.

name: VLAN Trunking Protocol Mismatch
issue: VLAN Database Out of Sync (VTP Mismatch)
possible_causes: Mismatched VTP domain, passwords, or revision numbers
remediation: Configure correct matching VTP domain name and password or change VTP mode to transparent/off.

name: Control Plane Storm Detected
possible_causes: Spanning Tree Loop or broken L2 loop prevention protocol
remediation: Verify spanning tree status, look for blocking ports, enable storm-control on switch interfaces.

name: NTP Time Out of Sync
possible_causes: NTP server unreachable or security firewall blocking UDP port 123
remediation: Add a reachable NTP server under 'ntp server <ip>' and check UDP 123 firewall rules.

name: EtherChannel PAgP/LACP Mismatch
possible_causes: Mismatched active/passive mode or mismatched protocols on channel group
remediation: Match EtherChannel protocol on both switches. Set both ports to active LACP using 'channel-group 1 mode active'.

name: DHCP Helper Address Missing
issue: Client IP Request Drop (DHCP Helper Missing)
possible_causes: Missing 'ip helper-address <dhcp_server_ip>' command on L3 interface serving clients
remediation: Add the DHCP server target under the L3 SVI or physical interface that is the gateway for client hosts.

name: Local Enable Secret Missing
possible_causes: Router privileged exec access is not protected by encrypted secret
remediation: Configure an encrypted enable password using the 'enable secret <password>' command.

name: OSPF Interface Network Type Mismatch
issue: OSPF Neighbors Stuck in 2-WAY (DR/BDR elected when not expected)
possible_causes: One side is configured as OSPF point-to-point and the other side as broadcast
remediation: Align the network type on both sides using 'ip ospf network broadcast' or 'ip ospf network point-to-point'.

name: Spanning Tree Bridge Loop
possible_causes: Spanning Tree is disabled on a VLAN containing redundant bridge links
remediation: Enable spanning-tree globally for the affected VLAN using 'spanning-tree vlan <vlan_id>'.

name: High Core Temperature Warning
possible_causes: Faulty chassis fan tray or dust accumulation blocking exhaust vents
remediation: Replace faulty cooling fans, clean filters, or check climate control in rack server room.

name: Power Supply Module Failed
possible_causes: A hardware fault on one of the redundant power supplies
remediation: Replace defective hot-swappable power supply unit. Verify electrical grid input socket power source.

name: BGP AS Number Misconfigured
possible_causes: The local router configuration contains a mismatched remote-as number for the peer
remediation: Correct the remote-as assignment in 'router bgp' to match peer's local autonomous system number.

name: SNMP Unencrypted Protocol (v1/v2c)
possible_causes: Using SNMP version 1 or 2c which transmits data in cleartext
remediation: Upgrade monitoring deployment to SNMP version 3 which supports strong SHA/AES cryptographic auth and encryption.

name: Switchport Access VLAN Missing
possible_causes: Access port configured to use a VLAN that has been deleted or not created
remediation: Create the access VLAN in switch config mode using 'vlan <vlan_id>'.

name: Dynamic Overload ACL Missing
possible_causes: The Access Control List bound to the NAT statement does not exist or has no rules
remediation: Verify and define the standard ACL matching local IP subnets to be translated by dynamic PAT.

name: High Ping RTT Latency
possible_causes: Physical link congestion, heavy packet buffer queues, or control plane exhaustion
remediation: Deploy Traffic Shaping/Policing, increase link bandwidth, or isolate traffic hogging bandwidth.
